pairs.question: What is the primary focus of Bluegrass Unlimited magazine?
pairs.answer: Bluegrass Unlimited is a monthly music magazine dedicated to the furtherance of bluegrass and old-time musicians, devotees, and associates. It covers all phases of bluegrass and old-time music, including biographical articles, discographies, record and book reviews, concert and festival dates, interviews, classified ads, and songs.

pairs.question: When was Bluegrass Unlimited first published, and what was its initial format?
pairs.answer: Bluegrass Unlimited was first published in 1966. Its initial format was a typed, mimeographed 7– x 8½–inch booklet-like publication with a hand-drawn logo, priced at $3 per year.

pairs.question: How did Bluegrass Unlimited evolve in 1970 under new publishers Pete and Marion Kuykendall?
pairs.answer: In the fall of 1970, under new publishers Pete and Marion Kuykendall, Bluegrass Unlimited transitioned from an informal to a full-time operation. The magazine upgraded to a larger, standard format printed on glossy paper, and it is now produced in full color on high-speed web offset presses.

pairs.question: What role did Bluegrass Unlimited play in the revival of bluegrass music during its early years?
pairs.answer: According to David Freeman, owner of Rebel Records and County Records, Bluegrass Unlimited helped revive bluegrass music by spreading awareness and highlighting its artistic value during a time when the genre was at a low point in the 1950s. The magazine played a key role in bringing bluegrass out of bars and into broader recognition.

pairs.question: Which organizations is Bluegrass Unlimited affiliated with?
pairs.answer: Bluegrass Unlimited is a founding member of the International Bluegrass Music Association (IBMA).

pairs.question: What is the current status of Bluegrass Unlimited as of 2020?
pairs.answer: In 2020, the Bluegrass Music Hall of Fame & Museum acquired Bluegrass Unlimited, marking a significant milestone in its history.
